title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer flags that the list of MariaDB CR kinds (Database, User, Grant) is duplicated in `keystone_controller.go:255-274` and `reconcile_database.go:491-540`, risking drift if a fourth kind is added. They want it hoisted to a single package-level `mariaDBResourceCtors` var, iterated from both call sites.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 342b9d8 with Claude Code

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=0 warning=1 info=2 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## WARNING (1)

### W-001: Parallel MariaDB resource list duplicated across isFirstFinalizingObservation and finalizeDatabaseResources
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:255-274` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: detect-duplicated-parallel-resource-lists

**Problem**: The hard-coded list of MariaDB CR kinds (Database, User, Grant) exists twice: once in isFirstFinalizingObservation (keystone_controller.go:255-274) and once in finalizeDatabaseResources (reconcile_database.go:491-540). The sourcery-ai fix on this PR consolidated toDelete + toCheck within finalizeDatabaseResources but the same list re-appears in the sibling file. Adding a fourth MariaDB CR kind (e.g. Backup) requires remembering both sites; the sentinel would then silently undercount while the cleanup deletes the new kind — or vice versa — producing exactly the drift failure mode the pattern documents.

**Code**:
```
// keystone_controller.go — isFirstFinalizingObservation
for _, obj := range []client.Object{
    &mariadbv1alpha1.Database{},
    &mariadbv1alpha1.User{},
    &mariadbv1alpha1.Grant{},
} {

// reconcile_database.go — finalizeDatabaseResources
mariaDBResourceCtors := []func() client.Object{
    func() client.Object { return &mariadbv1alpha1.Database{} },
    func() client.Object { return &mariadbv1alpha1.User{} },
    func() client.Object { return &mariadbv1alpha1.Grant{} },
}
```

**Action Required**: Hoist mariaDBResourceCtors to a package-level var in reconcile_database.go (or a new finalizer.go) and iterate over it from both call sites. Single source of truth; both Delete/Get and sentinel Get stay in lockstep.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Extract a single package-level var mariaDBResourceCtors = []func() client.Object{ func() client.Object { return &mariadbv1alpha1.Database{} }, func() client.Object { return &mariadbv1alpha1.User{} }, func() client.Object { return &mariadbv1alpha1.Grant{} }, } in reconcile_database.go and have both isFirstFinalizingObservation and finalizeDatabaseResources iterate over it instead of redeclaring the list.
```

**Related**: I-002

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: Single FinalizingDatabase event per termination invariant is undertested
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go:1586-1628` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The sentinel contract, per both the code comment (keystone_controller.go:159-162) and the docs (docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md Events section), is 'emit exactly once per termination, suppress on subsequent requeues while MariaDB CRs remain Terminating.' The existing TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingDatabaseEvent runs Reconcile once and asserts the event is present; TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsDatabaseFinalizedEvent covers only the brownfield case where no CR was ever live. Neither exercises the multi-pass de-duplication path: a regression that re-emits FinalizingDatabase on every requeue would pass the suite.

**Code**:
```
TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingDatabaseEvent (keystone_controller_test.go:1586-1628) runs Reconcile once and asserts the event is present; no test invokes Reconcile twice to verify the single-emission invariant.
```

**Action Required**: Add a test that (a) seeds MariaDB CRs with a pending-delete finalizer, (b) calls Reconcile twice, and (c) asserts FinalizingDatabase occurs exactly once across the drained event channel.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a test TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingDatabaseEventOnlyOnce that seeds MariaDB Database/User/Grant CRs with a pending-delete finalizer (so they remain Terminating between passes), calls Reconcile twice against the same Keystone CR, drains the event recorder channel, and asserts exactly one FinalizingDatabase event was emitted across both passes.
```

---

### I-002: Finalizer re-hardcodes the MariaDB CR naming convention instead of reusing the builders
**File**: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:495-595` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: buildDatabase, buildUser, buildGrant are the authoritative source of MariaDB CR names (keystone.Name, keystone.Namespace). finalizeDatabaseResources (line 495, 515) and isFirstFinalizingObservation (lines 546-595) each reconstruct that name via client.ObjectKey{Name: keystone.Name, Namespace: keystone.Namespace}. If a future change to the builders ever diverges (name suffix, namespace override), the finalizer will silently look up the wrong key and declare cleanup done while the real CRs leak.

**Code**:
```
client.ObjectKey{Name: keystone.Name, Namespace: keystone.Namespace} is reconstructed in finalizeDatabaseResources (reconcile_database.go:495, 515) and isFirstFinalizingObservation (reconcile_database.go:546-595) rather than being derived from buildDatabase/buildUser/buildGrant.
```

**Action Required**: Either call buildDatabase(keystone).GetObjectMeta() (etc.) to derive names, or introduce a tiny mariaDBResourceKey(keystone) client.ObjectKey helper that both the create and delete paths use.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Introduce a helper func mariaDBResourceKey(keystone *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone) client.ObjectKey { return client.ObjectKey{Name: keystone.Name, Namespace: keystone.Namespace} } and use it from both the create path (buildDatabase/buildUser/buildGrant) and the finalizer lookups in finalizeDatabaseResources and isFirstFinalizingObservation so naming stays in one place.
```

**Related**: W-001

---

## Summary

Code is well-structured, thoroughly tested, and the documentation is exemplary — the finalizer lifecycle, brownfield semantics, and event contracts are all captured in docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md. The one real concern is F1: the sourcery-ai fix on this PR coined the 'duplicated parallel resource lists' project pattern but the same duplication now exists a second time between isFirstFinalizingObservation and finalizeDatabaseResources. F2 and F3 are nice-to-haves. No CRITICAL issues; no blockers for merge.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 1 |
| INFO     | 2 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Safe to merge after addressing F1 (consolidate the duplicated MariaDB resource list into a single package-level source of truth). F2 (multi-pass event de-duplication test) and F3 (centralize CR name derivation) are recommended follow-ups but not blocking.
